rulings.Case Title: IN RE APPLICATION OF: JOHN/JANE DOE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner John/Jane Doe'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Duces Tecum And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 6, 2019, Line 1, Petitioner John/Jane Doe'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Duces Tecum And For Monetary Sanctions As a motion to quash appears to remain pending in the Illinois court, John/Jane Doe's motion to quash and for monetary sanctions in our court is ordered off calendar until such time as the parties' notify our court that the Illinois court has ruled. This is at base an Illinois matter and we defer to the Illinois court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ERIC WHITEHEAD VS. RAYMOND F. BOURIS, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Confirm Arbitration, Motion To Reduce Arbiration Award To Judgment And Motion For Attorneys' Fees, Costs, And Expenses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 6, 2019, Line 6, PETITIONER ERIC WHITEHEAD's Motion To Confirm Arbitration, Motion To Reduce Arbiration Award To Judgment And Motion For Attorneys' Fees, Costs, And Expenses The arbitrator only recently issued his final award, complete with rulings on attorney fees, waiting time penalties and costs. Not considering all post-arbitration issues at once would be inefficient. Thus, hearing on all pending motions is continued to September 27, 2019. Raymond F. Bourhis Inc. may, if it desires, file a supplemental brief on or before September 13 of up to 10 pages that addresses the arbitrator's rulings on attorney fees, waiting time penalties and costs. Eric Whitehead may file a 10-page brief on those same topics on or before September 20. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: COMMUNITY TENANTS' ASSOCIATION ET AL VS. VALSTOCK MANAGEMENT COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Appointment Of Discovery Referee (Joinder)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 6, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANTS STEPHEN LEE, BROADWELL LLC's Motion For Appointment Of Discovery Referee (Joinder). Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to Sept 16, 2019 at 9:00am, per the request of the moving party. email dated 8/30/19. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KAMARI QUINONES VS. WESTFIELD GROUP LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Business Records Of Lady Foot Locker Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 6, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANTS WESTFIELD GROUP LLC, WESTFIELD, LLC's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Business Records Of Lady Foot Locker. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: THE COURT WILL ISSUE NO TENTATIVE RULING. THE PARTIES SHOULD APPEAR AT HEARING ON SEPT 6, 2019 AND BE PREPARED TO DISCUSS THE RELATIONSHIP, IF ANY BETWEEN DEFENDANT WESTFIELD GROUP LLC AND LADY FOOT LOCKER; HOW DOCUMENTS IN THE POSSESSION OF LADY FOOT LOCKER CAN CONSTITUTE PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS OF DEFENDANT WESTFIELD GROUP AND WHY THE HOLDING IN SIERRA VISTA HOSPITAL V. SUPERIOR COURT IS OR IS NOT CONTROLLING. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TERRY ANN MCINTOCH ET AL VS. BANK OF AMERICA N.A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Independent Mental Examination Of Plaintiff Terry Ann Mcintosh
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 6, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA N.A.'s Motion To Compel Independent Mental Examination Of Plaintiff Terry Ann Mcintosh. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Bank of America's Motion to Compel a Mental Examination of Plaintiff Terry Ann McIntosh is granted subject to the following limitations: (1) The scope of the examination will be limited to that necessary to allow the examining physician(s) to express an opinion as to Plaintiff's cognitive impairment (if any) in 2015 as well as her cognitive impairment (if any) at the time of her examination. (2) The ME may be conducted by the two doctors identified in the declaration of James A. Armontrout. (3) No third parties will be present during the examinations, but the will be recorded and the recording and all other documents utilized in the examinations and any report will be provided to counsel for Plaintiff no later than fifteen (15) days prior to trial or prior to the taking of a deposition of the examining doctors. (4) The examinations may be conducted over two days, with the maximum of four hours on any given day, (5) Any testimony beyond the scope set forth in (1) above may not be used at trial. (6) Counsel for the moving party should prepared an appropriate order, submit to counsel for Plaintiff for approval as to form and deliver a copy to the Court at the hearing on September 6, 2019. It appears from the pleadings and papers in this matter that Plaintiff is claiming that Plaintiff suffered from some cognitive impairment beyond that of an normal individual aged 75 and Defendant is entitled to challenge that assertion with expert testimony. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DENTAL FORCE, INC. ET AL VS. BEMDAD BEHNOOD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 6, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT BAMDAD BEHNOOD's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Defendant Bemdad Behnood's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the complaint's breach of contract cause of action is granted with leave to amend. The cause regards a partially completed nondisclosure agreement - exhibit A to the complaint. Behnood's "Employer" is said in the NDA to be "Evangelista Dental Group Inc.," which is not a plaintiff. Plaintiffs say they could plead assignment of the NDA, but the current complaint does not do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
